name: frontend-skills
description: React/TypeScript frontend + Go backend enforcement harness. 92 wired hooks (101 scripts) enforce patterns on every edit. Green != done: `test-warning-check` (local Bash PostToolUse) + `ci-warning-audit` (Stop, scans `gh run view --log`) block passing tests with DeprecationWarning / React `act()` / unhandled rejection / `@ts-ignore` noise. 51 skills from TDD through CI-green PR, including /diagnose feedback-loop-first 6-phase debugger + /triage multi-tracker (gh + acli) state machine + /steelman anti-sycophancy guard + /snyk-ux-security JS+Go per-path vuln sweep (exploitability-triage first gate, top-level-first upgrade ladder, React 18 pin, `govulncheck` for Go). 21 LLM failure modes (7 Karpathy single-agent + 14 MAST multi-agent, Cemri et al. NeurIPS 2025), OWASP + STRIDE, Core Web Vitals gate, bun.lock + yarn.lock parity, worktree isolation + branch safety, MCP ban with CLI redirect (~20x savings), agent-browser integration (~91% token reduction). 9 agents (3 plan-review hats + karpathy reference), 5 routines. Single-source manifest codegen. Opus 4.7 tuned. POSIX-friendly.

interface.shortDescription: React/TypeScript + Go enforcement harness — 92 hooks, 101 scripts, 51 skills, 9 agents, 5 routines
interface.longDescription: React/TypeScript frontend + Go backend enforcement harness. 92 wired hooks across 101 scripts enforce patterns on every edit, including accessibility, Tailwind, React Compiler, Zustand, TanStack Router, connect-query, env validation, strict TypeScript, unhappy paths, security, warnings, CI, and PR feedback completeness. 51 skills guide the development lifecycle from TDD through CI-green PR, including /diagnose, /triage, /steelman, /snyk-ux-security, and domain-model/ADR workflows. 9 agents support review and planning hats. 5 routines automate recurring workflows. Codex packaging maps every supported equivalent lifecycle event, including PermissionRequest and PostToolUse failure handling, while Claude packaging keeps the full hook surface.
defaultPrompt: Set up the frontend development lifecycle
Run the full quality gate on my changes
Review this PR and fix all findings

x-changelog.4.10.2: 2026-05-06 -- Codex hook parity pass: map Claude PostToolUseFailure into Codex PostToolUse, add a Codex PermissionRequest adapter that reuses approval-safe Bash/MCP deny guardrails, and regenerate Codex hooks so the plugin uses every supported Codex lifecycle event with an equivalent harness behavior.
x-changelog.4.10.1: 2026-05-06 -- Codex marketplace packaging fix: point Codex plugin hooks at a Codex-supported lifecycle file, regenerate .codex/hooks.json from the manifest, refresh stale install-surface copy (92 hooks / 100 scripts / 51 skills / 9 agents / 5 routines), and bump marketplace metadata for upgrade detection.
x-changelog.4.10.0: 2026-04-29 -- Sync mattpocock/skills (Apr 17 -> Apr 29). New `/diagnose` skill ported verbatim: feedback-loop-first 6-phase debug discipline (build loop -> reproduce -> 3-5 ranked falsifiable hypotheses -> instrument with `[DEBUG-prefix]` cleanup grep -> fix at correct seam + regression test -> cleanup + post-mortem hand-off to `/improve-codebase-architecture`); 10 ranked Phase-1 strategies; ships `scripts/hitl-loop.template.sh`. Merged `github-triage` + `triage-issue` into single `/triage` (multi-tracker: `tracker-github.md` via `gh issue`, `tracker-jira.md` via `acli jira workitem`); bug-investigation + TDD-fix-plan absorbed as sub-mode; 5 canonical state roles map to project-specific labels/statuses; AI-disclaimer required on every triage comment. Vague-prose domain-glossary + ADR awareness added to `/tdd`, `/improve-codebase-architecture`, `/zoom-out`, `/triage` -- soft-dep, degrades gracefully when no `CONTEXT.md`. Upstream content merged while preserving 3-hat fan-out: `improve-codebase-architecture/LANGUAGE.md` (canonical vocab + deletion test); `tdd/tests.md` (good vs bad tests); `domain-model/ADR-FORMAT.md` 'what qualifies' enriched. Skills 48 -> 51. +96 eval checks (test-triage, test-diagnose, test-domain-model-merge); test-tdd patched. Skipped per direction: caveman, git-guardrails, to-prd/to-issues, /setup-matt-pocock-skills explicit pointer.
x-changelog.4.9.0: 2026-04-23 -- Green != done warnings gate (+2 hooks, 89->91 wired / 98->100 scripts). `test-warning-check.sh` (PostToolUse Bash) scans passing `vitest`/`bun test`/`playwright`/`tsgo`/`biome`/`lint` output for curated warning patterns: Node `DeprecationWarning` + `ExperimentalWarning` + `(node:...) XxxWarning`, React `act()`/missing-key/DOM-nesting/prop-type/bad-setstate/non-boolean-attr, unhandled promise rejection / unhandled error, vitest `stderr |` during test, skipped-test counts, `@ts-expect-error` / `@ts-ignore`, `npm WARN deprecated`. Per-finding escape `// allow: test-warning` in test file with reason; env `TEST_WARNINGS_ALLOW=1`. 3x same-kind streak escalates via systemMessage. `ci-warning-audit.sh` (Stop) fetches `gh run view <id> --log` on green PR CI (per-SHA cache, 45s timeout), greps same pattern, warns with file:line sample + fetch-full command. Gates: feature branch, session touched code, PR exists, all checks SUCCESS (pending/fail defer to lifecycle-stop). Disable via `CI_WARNING_AUDIT=0`. CLAUDE.md Tests section adds green-not-done rule. tdd/SKILL.md `When Done` adds zero-warnings local + CI log audit checkboxes. Why: tests can be green while runtime spews deprecations / logs errors / leaks unhandled rejections -- noise rots into real bugs when the libs actually remove the API. Agent must clean before calling it done.
x-changelog.4.8.0: 2026-04-22 -- /steelman anti-sycophancy skill (opt-in only). New skill at steelman/SKILL.md argues strongest opposite case with repo evidence on user assertions -- factual/causal/architectural claim types only (preference/goal/scope declined). Procedure: identify claim -> fan-out evidence gather (grep, read, run) -> write counter-argument with file:line refs -> verdict (confirmed | contradicted | mixed). User-invoked via `/steelman` slash only. **No ambient CLAUDE.md rule, no automatic hook fire, no telemetry log script** -- plugin consumers see zero behavior change unless they type `/steelman`. Ambient premise-check directive + jsonl telemetry were considered and held back pending real-world feedback on the opt-in surface; re-evaluate once /steelman fire-rate data exists from dogfooding. Why: LLM sycophancy risk when the user asserts something wrong and the model agrees by default -- /steelman forces the strongest counter-case with repo evidence before proceeding. Skills 47 -> 48 (registers the skill file that was previously on disk but unlisted).
